events.title: New car savings
events.description: You decide to build a dedicated pot for a new car.
descriptions: You commit to setting aside a chunk of savings for a new car payment.
A reliable ride becomes a priority—you redirect cash flow toward a vehicle fund.
Your old car is on borrowed time; you start funding a replacement.
Dealer brochures appear on the kitchen table. Time to save for wheels.

events.title: Uninsured medical bill
events.description: An injury or procedure lands a bill your plan does not fully cover.
descriptions: An ER visit and follow-ups arrive with a shockingly large invoice.
Physical therapy and imaging stack up—and insurance leaves a big gap.
A sports injury needs surgery; the deductible and out-of-network share hurt.
You’re fine now, but the hospital billing department wants payment yesterday.

events.title: Family needs help
events.description: Someone close needs financial support right away.
descriptions: A parent falls behind on rent—you step in to bridge the month.
Your sibling’s car died; they need a loan you feel you can’t refuse.
A family health scare means travel and hotels on short notice.
The group chat turns serious: relatives need cash for legal fees.

events.title: Urgent home repair
events.description: The house demands money before small problems become disasters.
descriptions: The roof leaks after a storm; contractors quote a painful number.
The heating system dies in winter. Replacement can’t wait.
Mold remediation and drywall turn a corner of your budget inside out.
Insurance covers part, but the deductible and upgrades still sting.

events.title: Tax bill surprise
events.description: You owe more to the tax authority than you expected.
descriptions: Your accountant delivers bad news: you underpaid through the year.
Side income and missing withholdings show up on the final return.
A prior-year adjustment arrives—you owe back taxes plus interest.
Estimated payments fell short; April (or the deadline) hits hard.

events.title: Big life celebration
events.description: A wedding, milestone trip, or major celebration needs funding.
descriptions: You’re in the wedding party—and flights, gifts, and attire add up fast.
A dream trip finally gets dates; deposits are due now.
A milestone anniversary deserves more than a card and dinner.
Celebration plans balloon; you want to participate without maxing cards.

events.title: Education expense
events.description: Course fees or certification hit before aid arrives.
descriptions: A certification program requires tuition this semester—no loans lined up.
Your kid’s program has a fee spike; payment is due in weeks.
You enroll in a bootcamp; the invoice is real and immediate.
Books, software, and lab fees stack on top of tuition.
